message:
fix(fj-lax): harden Split equal-section path against usize overflow

Address frankenjax-fuvh: eval_split() in crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_ops.rs
unconditionally computed row-major strides at the very top of the
function (before the equal-vs-unequal section branch), and then —
inside the unequal-section path — multiplied them with axis sizes
and section sizes using plain * arithmetic. With pathologically
large dims like Shape{ dims: vec![0, u32::MAX, u32::MAX, u32::MAX] },
those products overflow usize on 64-bit hosts and either silently
wrap (release builds) or panic (debug/test builds), instead of
returning a typed EvalError.

This change makes three coupled fixes:

1. Move the stride computation INSIDE the unequal-section arm
   (immediately before the first multiplication). The equal-section
   reshape path doesn't use strides at all, so it now gracefully
   returns the reshape result for huge zero-element tensors without
   ever touching the overflow-prone stride arithmetic.

2. Replace plain `*` with checked_mul / checked_add at every
   multiplication or addition that combines u32 dim values into
   usize offsets:
   - elements_per_slice = strides[axis] * section_size
   - axis_stride_span   = strides[axis] * axis_size
   - base               = outer * axis_stride_span
   - element index      = base + i
   Each overflow now returns EvalError::Unsupported with a precise
   "split <which> overflows usize" detail.

3. Replace `tensor.elements[index]` with `tensor.elements.get(index)`
   so an out-of-range index — which can happen on malformed inputs
   regardless of overflow — yields a typed EvalError instead of an
   index-out-of-bounds panic.

Outer-product computation `dims[..axis].iter().product()` is also
routed through the existing checked_shape_element_count helper for
consistency with the rest of fj-lax.

Add a regression test in crates/fj-lax/src/lib.rs (new
test_split_empty_huge_equal_section_returns_empty_tensor) that
constructs a 4-D zero-element tensor with axis dims of u32::MAX,
asks Split to slice it into one section along axis 0, and asserts
the result is an empty 5-D tensor whose dims propagate correctly —
exercising the equal-section reshape path without ever touching the
overflow-prone stride math.

Bump the test-count badge in README.md from 3373 → 3374 (one new
test added) and file the corresponding bead frankenjax-fuvh
("Harden split empty equal-section stride path") in
.beads/issues.jsonl as in_progress.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
